'Ndrina Critelli.description: The Critelli are a 'ndrina (mafia clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Cariati, Calabria, active in the provinces of Cosenza, Emilia-Romagna, and Germany. They engage in extortion, money laundering, drug trafficking, usury, and murder. They are allied with other 'ndrine from the Alto Ionio area. The clan has been targeted in several police operations including Galassia, Reset 2, Pitbull, Rheinbrücke, and Stige.

descriptions: The 'Ndrina Carelli is allied with the Critelli di Cariati.
edges.evidence_quote: Sono alleati con gli zingari di Lauropoli e con i Critelli di Cariati.

descriptions: 'Ndrina Critelli is a subunit of the 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: I Critelli sono una cosca malavitosa o 'ndrina della 'ndrangheta calabrese di Cariati

descriptions: 'Ndrina Critelli is allied with the 'ndrine of the upper Ionian coast.
edges.evidence_quote: Sono alleati con le 'ndrine dell'alto Ionio ( Cirò , Corigliano Calabro )

Pink Panthers.description: The Pink Panthers are an international jewel thief network with roughly 800 core members, many of whom are ex-soldiers with military backgrounds. Founded by Dragan Mikić in Belgrade, Serbia, the group is named by Interpol after the Pink Panther films. They are responsible for audacious heists across multiple countries and continents, including Japan's most successful robbery. The organization remains active as of 2001–present.

descriptions: YACS evolved into Pink Panthers.
edges.evidence_quote: Interpol believes that the YACS Crime Group that evolved into The PINK PANTHERS are responsible for US$ 130 million in bold robberies

Puente 13.description: Puente 13 is a Sureño street gang based in La Puente, California, founded around 1953 as the Bridgetown Gentlemen. It is affiliated with the Mexican Mafia and engages in drug trafficking, fraud, murder, and assault. The gang claims the entire city of La Puente and operates in the San Gabriel Valley.

Los Charlines.description: A Galician drug trafficking clan founded in 1975 by Manuel Charlín Gama and his family. Based in Galicia, Spain, they engaged in smuggling, hashish and cocaine trafficking, and money laundering. The clan was dismantled in 1990 by judge Baltasar Garzón but remained active, with members imprisoned and later released. They are considered one of the most important drug clans in Galicia.

The Johnson Gang.description: A group of Romanichal criminals from Cheltenham, Gloucestershire, who specialized in stealing fine art and antiques from English country houses over 20 years. The gang comprised Ricky Johnson, his sons, and associates, and their thefts are estimated to be worth between £30 million and £80 million. They were sentenced to a total of 49 years in prison in August 2008.

A.F.C.A.description: A Dutch hooligan firm linked to AFC Ajax, based in Amsterdam. It consists of multiple subgroups such as F-side, VAK410, and others, which cooperate during riots. The firm is involved in football hooliganism, riots, fighting, arson, and drug trafficking. It has alliances with hooligan groups from Anderlecht, Maccabi Tel Aviv, and Cracovia, and rivals with Feyenoord-related firms.

La Corporación.description: Bolivian drug cartel and criminal organization founded in the 1970s by Roberto Suárez Gómez. It became one of the world's largest cocaine producers, especially after the 1980 coup d'état that gave it political protection. The cartel supplied the Medellín Cartel and was active until the early 1990s.

edges.evidence_quote: Because of the relationship with Suárez and Pablo Escobar, "La Corporación" became a major supplier to the Medellín Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: They were allied with several Mexican and Colombian drug cartels
